sources.title: Mem0 Memory Evaluation
sources.excerpt: The key architectural decision is ADD-only extraction. New facts are stored alongside old ones — nothing is overwritten or deleted. This preserves temporal context and eliminates information loss from premature consolidation.

sources.title: Letta Memory Blocks
sources.excerpt: Memory blocks are structured sections of the agent’s context window that persist across all interactions. They are always visible - no retrieval needed.

sources.title: Letta Archival Memory
sources.excerpt: Archival memory is a semantically searchable database where agents can store facts, knowledge, and information for long-term retrieval. Unlike memory blocks, archival memory fragments cannot be pinned to the context window, and must be queried on-demand via tools.
